titleStmt.title: SII 25.138: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 138. (A.R. No. 138 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab lying in the Aṅgāḷammaṉ temple.
This record is dated in a year which seems to be ‘four’ in numerals in the reign period of the above king.

It registers a gift of 150 sheep for the maintenance of a perpetual lamp to the deity Jalaśayaṉattu-bhaṭārar of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Koḻuvūrk-kūṟṟam by Maṉōmayaṉ-Māṟaṉ, a kāvidi of Iḷaveṇbaikkalattirukkai in Veṇbaikkuḍi-nāḍu. It was required to supply one uri of ghee daily for the lamp. A body called Āyiratteḻunūṟṟuvar is mentioned as the protector of the endowment. The word kāvidi is an official title which is being applied to the officials both in the Pāṇḍya and in the Chōḷa Country. Maṉōmayaṉ is probably a variant of the expression ‘Manōnmaṇi’, for e.g., Maṉōmayanīchchurattu used as the name of a deity in a record of the 2nd year of Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ (See S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 17).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 25.138 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
